LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JI YANG, ALELI SAN JUAN, IRMANETTE DE ROSAS, MILENN MARIANO AND MARLYN DATAR'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE COSTS TO EXPEDITE PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION BECAUSE, IN THE ABSENCE OF ANY CONTRARY EVIDENCE PRODUCED BY PLAINTIFFS, DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE IS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT THIS WAS A NECESSARY COST. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE PROCESS FEES OF $388. PLAINTIFF RAISED AN ISSUE AS TO THE PROPRIETY OF THESE CHARGES AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO ADDRESS OR EVEN ARGUE IN OPPOSITION THAT THE CHARGES WERE ACCURATE AND PROPER. MOTION IS MOOT AS TO THE $90 CHARGE FOR TRANSCRIPTS AS THE REVISED MEMORANDUM OF COSTS EXCLUDES THE ITEM. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE CHARGES FOR MESSENGER SERVICES. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION STATING THAT THE CHARGES WERE NECESSARY FOR NUMEROUS REASONS IS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO FIND THE CHARGES NECESSARY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS - MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. GIVEN THE LACK OF EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN CONNECTION WITH THE INSTANT MOTION, THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS WERE "FRIVOLOUS, UNREASONABLE OR GROUNDLESS." (ROSENMAN V. CHRISTENSEN, MILLER, FINK, JACOBS, GLASER, WEIL & SHAPIRO (2001) 91 CAL.APP.4TH 859, 868; SEE ALSO JERSEY V. JOHN MUIR MED. CTR. (2002) 97 CAL.APP.4TH 814, 831 [DEFENDANT NOT ENTITLED TO AWARD OF FEES AND COSTS MERELY BECAUSE SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS GRANTED IN ITS FAVOR].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185 =302/EG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Deft'S Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-27-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL RAY BACON, TEL. NO. 864-0907 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Defendant's Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs is denied. This Motion cites the same evidence as Defendant's original motion for attorneys' fees, and cites no new facts, circumstances, or law, as required under CCP section 1008(a). The Court relied on all evidence properly submitted with Defendant's original motion, and the failure of Defendant to provide to the Court additional evidence which it would have liked the Court to consider does not establish a "new circumstance." Furthermore, Defendant's request in the alternative that the Court grant the Motion for Reconsideration pursuant to CCP section 473(b) is denied as Defendant seeks relief not available under CCP section 473(b). That code provision permits relief from a prior order; it does not permit the Court to modify a prior order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL RAY BACON, TEL. NO. 864-0907 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ALAN S. LEVINS OF LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 677-3156 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JI YANG, ALELI SAN JUAN, IRMANETTE DE ROSAS, MILENN MARIANO and MARLYN DATAR's Motion To Tax Costs is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is denied as to the costs to expedite Plaintiff's deposition because, in the absence of any contrary evidence produced by Plaintiffs, Defendant's evidence is sufficient to establish that this was a necessary cost. The Motion is granted as to the process fees of $388. Plaintiff raised an issue as to the propriety of these charges and Defendant failed to address or even argue in opposition that the charges were accurate and proper. Motion is moot as to the $90 charge for transcripts as the revised Memorandum of Costs excludes the item. Motion is denied as to the charges for messenger services. Counsel's declaration stating that the charges were necessary for numerous reasons is sufficient evidence to find the charges necessary. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL RAY BACON, TEL. NO. 864-0907 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ALAN S. LEVINS OF LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. 677-3156 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME's Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs - Motion for Attorneys' Fees and Costs is denied. Given the lack of evidence presented in connection with the instant Motion, the Court is unable to determine whether Plaintiffs' claims were "frivolous, unreasonable or groundless." (Rosenman v. Christensen, Miller, Fink, Jacobs, Glaser, Weil & Shapiro (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 859, 868; see also Jersey v. John Muir Med. Ctr. (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 814, 831 [defendant not entitled to award of fees and costs merely because summary judgment was granted in its favor].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/eg

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Continued To Apr-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Apr-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Ray Bacon, Esq., 415.864.0907, for plaintiff Ji Yang.
Alan Levins, Esq., and Andrea Milano, Esq., 415.433.1940, for defendant Hebrew Home for the Aged, Disabled DBA Jewish Home.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant presents a legitimate business reason for the terminations based on the declaration of Dr. Cabigao. The evidence indicates that even though Dr. Cabigao conferred with others, he was the ultimate decisionmaker. Therefore, to avoid summary judgment, plaintiffs must present substantial responsive evidence showing that Dr. Cabigao's reasons were untrue or pretextual. See Hersant v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-1005. Plaintiff fails to meet that burden. The undisputed evidence shows that: (1) Dr. Cabigao is in the same class as plaintiffs (Asian) and there is an inference against racial discrimination in such circumstances; (2) Dr. Cabigao had a role in hiring plaintiff Yang and there is an inference against discrimination where the hirer and firer are the same person; (3) the nurses that replaced plaintiffs are Asian; and (4) seventy-five percent of the nurses that work for defendant are Asian. Lastly, plaintiff contends that defendant acted discriminatorily because Ms. Serafin and Ms. Newman, who treated Resident X, were not terminated or even investigated. The evidence shows that Ms. Serafin and Ms. Newman did not see Resident X as frequently as plaintiffs and plaintiffs' theory of disparate treatment founders because the plaintiffs on the one hand, and Ms. Serafin and Ms. Newman, on the other hand, were not similarly situated. See Wills v. Sup. Crt. (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 143. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Angie Diner 581
